In the poker scenario, the author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck. To calculate the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards, we use the complement rule. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from the deck is C(52, 5). The number of ways to choose 5 cards without any hearts is C(36, 5). The probability of getting at least one heart is 1 minus the probability of getting no hearts, which is 1 - [C(36, 5) / C(52, 5)]. This simplifies to a closed form: 1 - [C(36, 5) / C(52, 5)].

For Strategy B, the expected value is 0.6 * 800 + 0.4 * (-200) = 480 - 80 = 400. The author should choose Strategy B, as it has a higher expected value.
